evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: metrics depend on well-modeled, reliable data
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — reliable data, data governance, quality and performance, well-modeled — with no velocity phrases present; classified as rigour.
evidence.domain_risk: IT Services and IT Consulting; end client unnamed
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: IT consulting with unnamed end client and no financial or regulatory language in JD; errors affect business decisions without regulatory consequence; classified as moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Collaborate with Data Engineers on quality and performance; data as a product
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Distinct Data Engineer role implies multiple data team members; 'data as a product' mindset and named tools in requirements suggest an established growing data function; classified as mid.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design and maintain BI solutions including reports, dashboards, and data models for decision-making; Build and optimize ETL/ELT processes for data availability
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities are generic boilerplate applicable to any BI role — no named tools in responsibilities, no scale numbers, no specific methodology; classified as recruiter.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: data as a product; metrics depend on well-modeled, reliable data; data governance and documentation
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary emphasis on the data layer itself — well-modeled data, data as a product, governance — serving data consumers rather than a named business function; classified as internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Define KPIs with business stakeholders; Support data governance and documentation
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Mix of defining KPIs (strategic) and supporting governance and collaborating with Data Engineers (execution); neither fully dominates; classified as mixed.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design and maintain BI solutions; Enhance reporting solutions focusing on usability and performance
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Designing new solutions (greenfield) alongside enhancing and maintaining existing reporting (fix_scale) with neither dominant; classified as mixed.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD lists standard BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker) and ETL/ELT skills with no mention of AI coding tools or GenAI-consuming infrastructure.
evidence.testing_framing: Collaborate with Data Engineers on quality and performance
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Quality is framed as a shared collaborative activity with Data Engineers rather than an owned responsibility with an explicit ownership verb, matching the absent classification.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: metrics depend on well-modeled, reliable data; data governance and documentation
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability and governance concerns are secondary to the JD's overall BI delivery focus and carry no compliance or regulatory language, consistent with moderate loss aversion.
